**What it is:** Daily 10–15 minute lesson opener for AP English Language & Composition teachers. Two auto-generated versions (student + teacher) delivered M–F at 6am local. One-time 4-minute setup form. Zero manual work per teacher after setup.

**Target customer:** ~10,000 U.S. high school AP Lang teachers. Spends 8–15 hrs/week on article prep. Already pays out of pocket for EdTech tools. Active on Facebook AP Lang groups and AP Lang subreddit.

**The daily artifact:**
- Student version: 350–450 word nonfiction article (current within 7 days), 2 discussion questions, 2–3 AP-style MC (no answers), 1 writing prompt (Q1/Q2/Q3, rotates per D-001)
- Teacher version: all above + rhetorical devices pre-marked with para/line refs, MC answer key, sample strong responses, common misconceptions, AP exam alignment notes
- Delivery: email (default) + web link + Google Doc + PDF

**Pricing:** $19.99/mo solo, $190/yr annual, $14.99/seat/mo dept (5+ seats annual), "Contact us" for school/district. 14-day free trial, no card required. One plan, no Pro tier.

**Key decisions locked:**
- AP Lang only in 2026 (not AP Lit, not other subjects)
- Dual-version output by default, no toggle
- 350–450 word article target
- 2 discussion questions per brief
- Writing prompt framed as homework/extended writing, not part of opener
- Annual billing default + summer-as-feature (no pause option)
- No Pro tier in year 1
- D-001 (May 2, 2026): AP unit handling = mixed-by-default rotation (Q1/Q2/Q3), teacher-version footer shows current state with one-click lock override. "Current AP unit" setup field now optional with "skip if cycling" framing.
- D-004 (May 22, 2026): AP Lang canon (famous/commencement speeches: JFK, MLK, Wallace's Kenyon, Saunders, FDR, etc.) stays OUT of the default content cadence. Reaffirms D-002 item (3): September pilot ships current nonfiction, open/CC-commercial/public-domain, 350-450 words. Reasoning: teachers have no prep pain with the canon (already own it, public domain, curriculum guides spoon-feed it); mixing it in dilutes the "current within 7 days" value prop and pulls toward a different product. Canon is a post-MVP expansion lever ("Speech of the Week" Friday slot, or a classic thematically paired with the week's article) on the same delivery rails, and/or a discovery-interview probe ("When you teach rhetorical analysis with historical speeches, what's the friction there?") to confirm demand before building.
- D-002 (May 13, 2026): Build-readiness session. Locked 7 decisions: (1) Samuel is the quality bar, AP Lang reviewer only on a defined trigger; (2) hallucination control = sample-based audit (position 2); (3) article sourcing = open/CC-commercial/public-domain only (option D); (4) format-drift = hybrid now, regression suite at ~200 teachers; (5) editorial neutrality = 3-tier framework draft; (6) ops = Claude support agent post-MVP + optimized infra targeting ~95% margin; (7) timeline reframe (see below). Discovery script revised → Product Brief v1.1. Core throughline: build risk is a 2027 scale-phase problem; September 2026 is a sales/evidence problem solvable with zero code.

**Timeline (revised by D-002, May 13 — calendar-collision fix):**
- ✅ Lock spec v1.0 — April 27, 2026; v1.1 (discovery script revision) — May 13, 2026
- Task #2: Teacher-facing one-pager (paused, see below)

- Jul → mid-Aug: NO classroom (school out). Concept/sample validation, collect pre-commitments, build safe delivery rails, recruit fall pilot cohort
- Mid/late Aug → September: Manual MVP runs for real (4 weeks, hand-written briefs in live classrooms)
- September: arrive at Northwestern with spec + interviews + live pilots + pre-commitments (NOT converted paying users — calendar makes that impossible before October)
- October: convert pilots to paying
- ⚠️ Samuel's homework: verify the Wildfire fall-2026 application deadline. If spring 2027, calendar collision evaporates and timeline relaxes.

**Why:** Samuel wants The Garage evidence stack by September 2026. The Caught Up AI vehicle satisfies three criteria: concrete measurable pain, workflow-based moat, solo-buildable MVP phase.

**Update 2026-06-04 - Product design session; CONTENT ENGINE is the open blocker:**
Began designing the product (end-to-end journey map; spine agreed; paused before deep-designing the Landing stage). The session went deep on where the daily article comes from, which gates everything.
- D-005: Link / point-to-source model REJECTED. The teacher version marks up rhetorical devices INSIDE the text, so the article must be reproduced AND modified in the email; cannot be done on a remote link.
- D-006 (finding, verified by deep research): To reproduce-in-full, annotate inline, and sell, only PUBLIC DOMAIN / CC0 / CC-BY licenses qualify. CC-BY-SA's ShareAlike makes the paid teacher version freely redistributable, so treat as off. ALL NonCommercial (NC) out (paid product); ALL NoDerivatives (ND) out (annotation = derivative). The Conversation = CC-BY-ND (out); ProPublica = CC-BY-NC-ND (out).
- D-007 (finding, verified by spot-check): The free CC-BY/PD pool of CURRENT, argument-driven, classroom-safe nonfiction is too thin for a daily cadence. Global Voices (CC BY 3.0) is the lone genuine current source (~1-2 usable/wk, activism-skewed). The 7-day vs 30-day window is irrelevant; the constraint is licensing structure, not recency.
- D-008: Canon-as-backbone REJECTED (reaffirms D-004). No teacher prep pain, no moat, kills the subscription logic, and the modern canon is still copyrighted. Canon stays a supplement only.
- AI-quality scope (honest): AI can write rhetorically rich, hard-to-detect, useful ANALYSIS TEXT, but only with a human editor per piece; it cannot fully replicate authentic pathos / lived experience or supply a real rhetorical situation. So AI-at-quality = AI + human editor (labor), plus trust/disclosure tradeoffs. Fully automated = spottable/hollow.
- OPEN DECISION (resume here): the content engine. Cannot keep real + not-commissioned + current + daily + cheap + annotatable (empty set); must relax one. Honest finalists: LICENSE real op-eds (spend money; annotation/derivative rights are hard to get) vs AI-WITH-EDITING (spend labor; authenticity/trust tradeoffs). Other relaxations: drop to a 2-3x/week free product, or commission writers.
- Reframe to keep: sourcing difficulty IS the moat; the apparatus is the real IP, worth most on fresh content nobody else has covered.
- NEXT STEP proposed: draft a blind teacher test (unlabeled AI brief vs real brief) for discovery to settle whether real authorship is load-bearing. Alternative: resume the journey map at Landing.
- Does NOT block the fall manual MVP; this is the weakest link, tested by the MVP/discovery, not a stopper.
- Also covered (reusable): build-and-cost path sketch (Phase 0 manual ~$0-20/mo; Phase 1 semi-auto Base44 + Resend + Stripe ~$50-150/mo; Phase 2 automated ~$150-400/mo run + ~$3-25k one-time build); ~95% margin realistic; content cost flat in user count; biggest underestimated build risk = email deliverability.
- Full detail: vault 20_Conversations/2026-06-04 - Product design and content sourcing.md; license filter: 30_Knowledge/Caught Up AI - content licensing filter.md; project state: 10_Projects/Caught Up AI/Content-Sourcing.md.

**Update 2026-06-04 (cont.) - Blind test, AI side built:**
- Built the source-article Writing Manual (10_Projects/Caught Up AI/Writing-Manual.md), reverse-engineered from the verified released AP Lang exam Q2 corpus (2005-2024): open/build/close spine, single structural engine, long-vs-short AND syntactic-variety rhythm rules, earned-device palette, AI-tell blocklist (no em-dashes, banned diction/constructions), markability test, paste-ready generation brief. This is the editorial standard / apparatus IP for the content engine.
- Decision: for the blind teacher test, Claude writes the AI side only; Samuel supplies currently-published real op-eds to pair, strips labels, randomizes order. Topic lane = safe + pointed (evergreen, non-partisan).
- Wrote 3 AI essays to the manual (passion-as-career-advice myth; optimization/hustle culture; true-crime ethics), each in its own Google Doc on the Northwestern account (samuellevy2030@u.northwestern.edu). Backup: 10_Projects/Caught Up AI/Blind-Test-Essays.md.
- Next: Samuel reviews/owns the essays, selects+pairs the real comparators, builds the scoring instrument (guess AI vs real + which they would rather teach), runs in discovery to settle license-vs-AI-with-editing.
- Corpus correction to remember: Chavez nonviolence passage = 2015 Q2 rhetorical analysis, not a 2014 argument prompt.
- Detail: 20_Conversations/2026-06-04 - AP Lang writing manual and blind-test essays.md.

**Update 2026-06-04 (cont. 2) - Content-engine cost research; commissioning emerges as front-runner:**
- Ran cost research on the three relaxations (3 parallel research agents, sourced). LICENSING: price is minor, rights are fatal for major publishers (verbatim-only standard licenses; annotation = derivative = bespoke enterprise/legal deals + moral-rights gate; not solo-obtainable). Newsela is the only commercial reproduce+modify precedent (pays 175+ publishers, venture-funded; not copyable). Only surviving license channel = direct from independent/Substack writers who own their rights (~$150-600/piece; ~$1,650/mo at 2-3x/wk). FREE POOL: reduced cadence does NOT fix supply; only Global Voices (CC BY, ~1-3/wk) plus US-federal public-domain texts (rhetorically great, current, but partisan = neutrality risk). Honest free cadence: 1x/wk comfortable, 2x/wk strained, 3x/wk breaks.
- COMMISSION ORIGINALS (work-for-hire) promoted to front-runner: solves real authorship + full ownership/annotation + currency + classroom neutrality at once; ~$100/piece mid (400-600 wds), ~$860/mo (2x/wk) to ~$1,300/mo (3x/wk), flat in user count, break-even ~65 subs. Proof model: The Juice (original journalist/educator content, leveled, 6am, no verbatim reposting). Reduced cadence's real value is making commissioning affordable pre-revenue, not unlocking the free pool.
- Reframed finalists: commission originals (2-3x/wk) + free/PD/canon supplement on off-days is the front-runner; AI-with-editing is now the cost-saver variant of commissioning (today's formula critique weakens pure-AI); licensing survives only as direct-from-writers, which commissioning dominates. Blind-test question shifts to "does authentic authorship move teachers at all?"
- Feedback logged: rule-driven AI prose reads formulaic across pieces (feedback_ai_writing_formulaic.md).
- OPEN next step: design the commissioning pipeline (writer sourcing, work-for-hire contract, per-piece brief from the Writing Manual, budget) vs run the blind test first. Detail: 10_Projects/Caught Up AI/Content-Sourcing.md (2026-06-04 cost research section).

**Update 2026-06-04 (cont. 3) - commissioning OUT; "leave the article untouched" idea (candidate D-009):**
- Samuel ruled out commissioning writers.
- New idea: reproduce the article verbatim (untouched) and put rhetorical-device locations in a SEPARATE apparatus in the teacher brief, instead of inline markup. So the product needs only REPRODUCTION rights, not DERIVATIVE rights, dissolving the core blocker (D-006). Licensing moves from structurally impossible to a price/terms question.
- Preliminary read: unlocks mainstream reprint/syndication licenses (verbatim "as written" now fits, so AP/Reuters/Tribune/reprints are a cost question not a rights wall) and reopens CC-BY-ND sources (verbatim commercial repro allowed; article + separate apparatus = collection, not derivative); prize = The Conversation if its terms allow paid use. Does NOT fix NonCommercial (still out) or make reproduction free. Small pedagogy tradeoff (device map beside clean article).
- To verify: the derivative-vs-collection line (actual terms + a lawyer; keep article/apparatus physically separate); ND contract overlays (The Conversation is the swing factor); reprint pricing at cadence. Samuel asked whether to run this verification.
- Detail: 10_Projects/Caught Up AI/Content-Sourcing.md (2026-06-04 "later" section).

**Update 2026-06-04 (cont. 4) - D-009 VERIFIED (31-agent workflow): sound with conditions, free-daily dream is dead:**
- Legal model = SOUND WITH CONDITIONS: verbatim article + physically separate, non-interleaved apparatus = a collection not a derivative (17 USC 106(2)); well-reasoned but UNTESTED on these facts -> needs a written copyright-attorney opinion before launch (hard gate). Reproduction/distribution rights still engaged; fair use does not rescue full verbatim commercial copying.
- Opens new avenues but narrow/conditional: CC-BY-ND for verbatim paid reuse, standard reprint licenses become the right shape (but unaffordable), direct-from-writer licensing. THE CONVERSATION + KNOWABLE STILL CLOSED on a separate CONTRACT overlay (no selling separately / no systematic republishing) the design can't fix; The Conversation also mandates a tracking pixel (student-privacy). Newly-clean CC-BY-ND pool is thin: Singularity Hub (provisional) + OpenMind (conditional). Still closed: ProPublica (NC), NYT etc (terms), SA.
- Pricing: free-in-cash = federal PD (only confirmed base) + Global Voices (contact-gated, Left) + provisionals; real cost = curation labor ~5-10 hrs/wk. Paid all poor for small budget (AP ~$1,600/mo smallest, Tribune high-hundreds-thousands, NYT/WaPo/WSJ ~$9-20k/yr daily, Reuters enterprise, CCC rail-only). Direct-from-writer only one that scales to 1/wk; price TBD by blind test. Unit econ: ~$14 net/sub, ~16-46 subs fund one writer-piece/wk.
- 3-5/wk? NOT on free alone: honest floor ~2/wk, 3 typical, 4-5 rare; four-filter squeeze; only federal PD confirmed today. Free + ~1 paid writer/wk -> steadier 3-4. Reliable 5-day not deliverable free. Don't promise cadence until a 2-4 wk real pull counts survivors.
- Recommended engine: federal PD base + gated free (Global Voices, Singularity Hub) + OpenMind upside + ~1 direct-from-writer piece/wk later. Format discipline load-bearing (bit-for-bit article, zero inline labels, separate apparatus, short-phrase quotes by location).
- OPEN next steps (ordered): (1) volume pressure-test FIRST; (2) clear 3 free gates; (3) blind-test writer channel for prices; (4) scope state/local + institutional CC0; (5) apparatus template; (6) lawyer opinion = launch gate. Samuel to pick which to start. Full memo: 10_Projects/Caught Up AI/Licensing-Verification.md.

**Update 2026-06-05 - Pivot toward full AI-generation; v2 prose-engine research prompt built:**
- Paid-writer / commissioning path treated as off (cost + the earlier ruling). Samuel leaning to FULL AI-GENERATION: Claude writes each piece from scratch off verified facts (facts are not copyrightable), NOT reproducing/paraphrasing source prose. This sidesteps the licensing wall (D-006/D-009 were all about reproducing others' text) and relocates the main risk to editorial: current-events accuracy + the AI-prose-reads-same-y problem (feedback_ai_writing_formulaic).
- Target clarified: emulate the PROFESSIONAL SOURCE passages students analyze on the AP Lang exam (Q2 rhetorical-analysis + Q1 synthesis sources), NOT student essays. Honest reframe: no single "AP Lang style"; the exam spans centuries/genres, so the target is a PALETTE of registers and success = blind-read indistinguishability across that palette. Emulate craft, not topics. Accuracy stays a separate hard requirement.
- Built a paste-ready deep-research COMMISSION via a 10-agent workflow (ground exam facts + real corpus -> draft through 4 lenses: rhetoric scholar / stylometry / AI-tells / variety-system -> adversarial critique -> synthesize). Saved at 10_Projects/Caught Up AI/AP-Lang-Research-Prompt.md (em-dashes stripped, ASCII-normalized).
- When run, it produces v2 of the Writing Manual: Style-Palette, Structural-Templates, Register-Specs (qualitative + stylometric bands), Anti-Tell-List, Cross-Piece-Sameness-Rubric, Annotated-Exemplars, Generation-Briefs, Blind-Test-Protocol, and a master Prose-Engine-Research-Report. Supersedes (does not overwrite) Writing-Manual.md v1, whose known weakness is a single template/rhythm rule that would itself become a detectable house style.
- OPEN next: Samuel skims 6 knobs (register count, corpus span, blind-test scale, piece length, detector reliance, exemplar topics), adjusts, then runs the commission from the vault folder. Conversation: 20_Conversations/2026-06-05 - AP Lang prose engine research prompt.md.

**Update 2026-06-05 (build) - v2 prose engine DELIVERED:**
- Ran the commission as a 56-agent ultracode workflow (~57 min, 3.2M subagent tokens). All 10 deliverables written to 10_Projects/Caught Up AI/: Style-Palette, Structural-Templates, Register-Specs, Anti-Tell-List, Cross-Piece-Sameness-Rubric, Accuracy-Guardrail, Annotated-Exemplars, Blind-Test-Protocol, Generation-Briefs, Prose-Engine-Research-Report. Writing-Manual.md v1 left intact with a supersession note at top.
- PALETTE (7 registers): The Ledger (impersonal evidence-forward anchor/default), The Open Letter (deferential periodic appeal to a named power), The Reckoning (civic indictment, charge to act), The Tribute (epideictic consecration, benediction close), The Long Think (essayistic reasoning to an earned turn; annexes controlled satire), The Witness Stand (first-person memoir-as-evidence), The Long Look (descriptive awe, withholds moral judgment). Distinctness audit hard-gated the two near-merge pairs (Reckoning/Tribute via close-move; Long Think/Long Look via commit-vs-render, Louv reassigned to Long Look).
- Corpus: 49 entries, provenance re-audited (zero false exam-confirmations found; all 3 v1 corrections held; minor date/slot refinements only). Stylometric bands independently recomputed for 4 public-domain registers and held (no band contradicted). One material claim correction logged (arXiv 2506.21817 mis-cited for connective overuse).
- PILOT IS A PRE-SCREEN ONLY, not a pass signal. Automated proxy went 7/7 identifying the machine piece, but that is CONFOUNDED: the exemplars still carry [PLACEHOLDER] tags (a dead giveaway) and topic/era differs from the genuine comparison passages. Band check: only 2/8 pieces fully in band (6 drift on rhythm/openers/length pole). Both are documented honestly in Blind-Test-Protocol.md sec 9. The decisive test is the HUMAN AP-Lang-teacher panel (protocol fully specified, Samuel to run), which requires placeholder-free (verified-fact) pieces and topic-matched pairs.
- Post-build fixes by Claude: removed one banned word ("leverage" noun -> "the upper hand") from the Witness Stand exemplar; confirmed all 10 files em-dash/emoji clean; added the supersession note to Writing-Manual.md.
- OPEN next (Samuel's call): (1) read Style-Palette + Register-Specs + Generation-Briefs + Annotated-Exemplars; (2) calibrate the 6 out-of-band exemplars back into their bands and feed fixes into Register-Specs/Anti-Tell-List (the iteration loop); (3) to run the real blind test, produce placeholder-free pieces with verified facts and recruit AP-Lang teachers (ties to existing teacher list/outreach tracker). Conversation: 20_Conversations/2026-06-05 - Prose engine v2 build.md.

**Update 2026-06-05 (Proxy Run 2 - cleaner proxy):**
- Ran a cleaner self-proxy (72-agent workflow): 14 placeholder-free pieces from the REAL Generation-Briefs (2/register), each paired with a MODERN register-matched genuine human passage, 3 blind AI judges/pair. Result: 36/36 (100%) the AI judge still identified the machine piece, SAME as Run 1 even with the placeholder + era confounds removed. So the leak is in the WRITING, not test hygiene. (Long Think not blind-read: its genuine-passage fetch failed; 12 pairs x 3 = 36.) Band check: only 2/14 in band; opener variety is the #1 miss (too subject-first across nearly every register). Full file: 10_Projects/Caught Up AI/Proxy-Pilot-Run-2.md.
- CONSENSUS TELLS (the real payoff, what the judges named): (1) manufactured symmetry (not-X-but-Y, stacked tricolons, fixed-beat anaphora); (2) tidy closing buttons / aphoristic kickers on nearly every paragraph (esp. negation-correction closers); (3) frictionlessness (no awkwardness, no wasted/off-thesis detail, uniform polish = the tell); (4) too-tidy invented specifics (round numbers, placeholder-y proper nouns); (5) over-engineered keyword loops / ring composition; (6) reader-directing signposting + labeled enumeration. Human-contrast textures the pieces LACK: disfluency, run-ons/comma splices, spoken asides, idiosyncratic coinages, load-bearing verifiable detail, tonal unevenness, taking a side or trailing off.
- KEY STRATEGIC READ: (a) the spec's own emphasis on LEGIBLE rhetorical architecture is partly manufacturing the symmetry that reads as machine, the fix is a deliberate FRICTION / ANTI-SYMMETRY layer, not more rules; (b) a frontier AI judge in forced-choice is likely near-saturated (catches almost everything) and is partly cued by complete-tidy-WRITTEN-piece vs spoken/excerpt genuine comparisons, so 100% AI-judge does NOT equal 100% human; (c) do NOT keep optimizing against the AI judge (overfitting risk, locked param P5) -- the AI-proxy has now delivered its value (the cue list). The product-relevant bar is indistinguishable-to-HUMANS, not beating a detector.
- NEXT: build the friction/anti-symmetry layer into Register-Specs + Anti-Tell-List from these six cues, fix opener variety and the per-register punctuation/sentence-type band misses, regenerate, then run the DECISIVE human AP-Lang teacher panel per Blind-Test-Protocol.md (the only instrument that can return near-chance). Conversation: 20_Conversations/2026-06-05 - Prose engine v2 build.md.

**Update 2026-06-05 (v2.1 friction layer + sample essays):**
- DONE (first half of the NEXT above): integrated the six Run-2 consensus tells into Anti-Tell-List.md as a read-first "Friction / Anti-Symmetry Layer" (v2.1) plus mechanical-pass steps 9-11. The six fixes: cap manufactured symmetry (limit not-X-but-Y and autopilot tricolons), kill the tidy closing-button reflex (let paragraphs end flat), require deliberate friction (one inert/off-thesis detail + one uneven turn per piece), make invented specifics un-tidy (no round numbers / placeholder-y names; in production these are real verified facts), drop over-engineered keyword loops / ring composition, cut reader-directing signposting and labeled enumeration. Note: did NOT yet push these into Register-Specs per-register, and did NOT regenerate the 7 exemplars in Annotated-Exemplars (still the v2 versions); those remain open.
- Produced 3 sample essays from the v2.1 manual (The Ledger = state right-to-repair law; The Reckoning = transit fare-hike accountability; The Witness Stand = first-gen student + financial-aid verification) in a Google Doc titled "Caught Up AI - Friction-Layer Sample Essays (2026-06-05)" for Samuel to review. Facts illustrative, not fact-checked.
- Caveat: the friction layer is directional (derived from the AI-judge proxy Run 2, not humans). The decisive test remains the human AP-Lang panel.
- HUMAN READ (Samuel, first human review of friction-layer output): ranked The Reckoning (transit fare-hike) best, The Witness Stand (first-gen student) second, The Ledger (Minnesota right-to-repair) weakest = "a little wordy and hard to read." Signal: the hotter/personal registers land; the impersonal Ledger workhorse reads wordy. NEXT-SESSION PRIORITY when pushing friction into Register-Specs: a Ledger readability pass (shorter sentences, less subordination, lower clause depth, plainer phrasing, cut the long periodic closer); readability matters most there since Ledger is the daily default.
- 2026-06-06: First full end-to-end opener test run. Three openers built from the specs and delivered to Google Drive (folder "Caught Up AI - Test Openers (2026-06-06)"): Totality (Long Look), The Books We Don't Read (Long Think), The Recycling We Were Sold (Reckoning). Each = student + teacher copy in one doc, with the corrected device conventions (yellow highlight, whole-device spans, concession tagged alone, controlled vocab), 2 reading MCQs + key (different AP skills, named distractor traps), discussion + samples, Q2/Q3 prompt, misconceptions, AP alignment, sourcing note. Ran on EVERGREEN subjects on purpose to isolate the prose engine from the live-sourcing gate; every fact flagged for verification per the Accuracy-Guardrail (no breaking-news fabrication). Mechanical anti-tell pass clean (0 em-dashes, 0 banned vocab). Tooling note: Drive connector can't make a formatted native Doc, so delivered as HTML files that open in Google Docs with highlight (see reference_drive_mcp_formatting). Real .docx copies also in Sample-Briefs/. Renderers: Sample-Briefs/render_opener_docx.py (source of truth) and render_opener_html.py. These three are candidates for the human AP-Lang panel.

**Update 2026-06-08 (Editorial-Standards: neutrality + reading-level guardrails):**
- Samuel flagged two content problems in the existing sample openers and asked they never recur: (1) DIVISIVE/PARTISAN pieces (an Open Letter to an unnamed "Mr. President" clearly = the sitting president, taking a strong stance against him) must stop, even for teachers on the civic-pieces tier; never attack a specific person or group; (2) UNEXPLAINED JARGON above a high schooler's common knowledge (the $35-cap Ledger used Medicare "Part D"/"Part B" with no gloss) must stop.
- FIX AT SOURCE: created 10_Projects/Caught Up AI/Editorial-Standards.md (D9) = two cross-cutting guardrails above every register, on every political-intensity tier. Guardrail 1 = neutrality firewall (no political side, no attack on a person/group; Open Letter addressee and Reckoning culprit may never be a politician/party/identifiable political figure -> route to The Ledger or drop; the neutral path always exists via Ledger). Guardrail 2 = plain-knowledge accessibility (every unexplained term must be HS common knowledge; gloss specialist terms in one in-line clause on first use or cut; NOT dumb-down-the-prose, governs assumed background knowledge/jargon only; don't over-explain genuine common knowledge).
- WIRED IN as Gate 0 (runs first): Generation-Briefs.md pre-flight now "Five gates" with Gate 0 editorial fit; caughtupai-sampleprompt-V1.md (binding-file list + HARD RULES block + TOPIC MODE neutral-angle instruction + gate count -> five); Register-Specs.md Open Letter + Reckoning each got a NEUTRALITY drift cue; Style-Palette.md "How to use" got a neutrality+plain-knowledge routing note.
- Memory: feedback_opener_neutrality.md + feedback_opener_reading_level.md (both in MEMORY.md index).
- STILL OPEN (existing artifacts violate the new rules, NOT yet regenerated): the partisan samples in Sample-Briefs/ -- the Open Letters "Decide What Your Oath Was For", "The Forty-Year Rule You Wrote", "The Principles You Already Signed", "The Purse You Promised to Guard"; the Reckonings "The $186 Billion Nobody Stopped", "The Papers on the Table", "The First State to Name the Man"; and the jargon Ledger "The $35 Cap and the People It Misses". These should be regenerated or pulled before any teacher demo. Conversation: 20_Conversations/2026-06-08 - Opener content guardrails.md.

**Update 2026-06-10 (FIRST TEACHER MEETING held; headnote feature, feedback issue #1):**
- The first teacher meeting happened (2026-06-08, Samuel's own AP Lang teacher). Went well, teacher very interested. Samuel is now refining the product from his feedback, going ONE ISSUE AT A TIME. This update = issue #1; more to come.
- ISSUE #1 (teacher's main concern): the prose quality is strong but reads IMPERSONAL. His reasoning: AP Lang is largely about reading the author's point of view / audience / purpose to get the underlying tone. Example he loved: an Eisenhower letter to the Allied forces (a window into how Eisenhower saw and addressed his troops). His fix: a short context blurb before each piece (who is speaking, to whom, why).
- DECISION (Samuel: "build what you recommended"): adopt a register-aware, AP-EXAM-STYLE HEADNOTE = one or two italic sentences naming the rhetorical situation (speaker/role, audience, occasion/purpose), on BOTH copies, written FIRST as a forcing function (not a post-hoc caption). Honest caveats surfaced and accepted: a headnote does NOT fix impersonal prose (that's the Proxy-Run-2 generation problem); the Eisenhower "real person revealed" payoff can't be replicated for invented personas (so NO fake bios); speaker/audience/purpose doesn't fit neutral registers (Ledger/Long Look get an occasion/context line instead).
- TWO HONESTY RULES (judgment calls, approved): (1) frame invented personas by ROLE and scenario ("an open letter from a small-town pharmacist to..."), never a fabricated NAMED person; name real subjects (a real person in a Tribute, real events in a Ledger) truthfully -- same as AP framing a fiction excerpt; (2) the headnote does NOT disclose AI authorship (that's a teacher/product-level matter; the student analyzes the situation as presented, like a constructed AP synthesis scenario). Samuel also said: use real AP Lang exam headnotes as the style guide.
- BUILT: new spec 10_Projects/Caught Up AI/Headnote-Spec.md (D10; form rules, the two honesty rules, neutrality inheritance from Editorial-Standards Gate 0, a register-by-register content table, the write-it-first forcing function, an AP-exam style guide + banned moves). Renderer Sample-Briefs/render_opener_v2.py: added optional `headnote` field, rendered italic (Times-Italic, `hnote` style) directly above the article on BOTH copies; backward-compatible so the 28 existing openers still render. VERIFIED by rendering the built-in Long Think sample + extracting text from both PDFs (headnote present on both). Generation wiring: Generation-Briefs.md (write-headnote-first step in How-to, a headnote slot in all 7 briefs' Fill-before-drafting, and a Gate 2 pre-flight check = the finished prose must deliver the speaker/audience/purpose the headnote promises); caughtupai-sampleprompt-V1.md (Headnote-Spec added to read-list + a HEADNOTE block in the OUTPUT order).
- OPEN: (a) issues #2+ from the meeting still to process; (b) demo PDFs not yet backfilled with headnotes (the 2026-06-08 meeting edition + the 28-piece openers.json) -- offered to backfill key demos or regenerate fresh, awaiting Samuel's call; (c) Google Docs export renderers (render_opener_html.py, render_opener_docx.py) need the same `headnote` field if Openers are delivered as Google Docs (PDF path done). Conversation: 20_Conversations/2026-06-10 - Teacher feedback, headnotes.md.

**Update 2026-06-10 (DELIVERY FREQUENCY customization decided; not built):**
- New feature direction: teachers choose how often and which days they get Openers (1-5 days/wk, specific weekdays, since AP Lang meets on fixed schedules). Samuel's open question was whether this needs a portal/login.
- DECIDED via 3 forks, all locked to the lean option: (1) MECHANISM = magic-link preferences page, NO portal/login. Schedule set on the existing 4-min signup form (default all 5 weekdays, deselect to taste); a signed "change my schedule" link in every email footer opens a no-password page (days, political dial, unsubscribe). Full login portal DEFERRED until several account-reasons co-exist at once (multi-seat/department mgmt, billing self-service, content archive). Frequency alone never justifies the auth support surface. (2) CONTENT MODEL = broadcast + edition-rolled rotation. One edition published per day; frequency is a receive-filter on which days a teacher gets it (content load/print-and-go/QA all unchanged). Register rotation advances by EDITION DATE not weekday, so a part-week teacher still cycles the whole palette over weeks. Rejected per-teacher streams (per-teacher queue state, multiplies QA, wrong for manual/semi-auto MVP). The Generation-Briefs rotation grid already specifies rolling advance when publishing <7 days, so this is config, not new work. (3) PRICING = one price, frequency is FREE (a churn/retention lever, not a tier; no cost basis since one edition serves all; avoids "$20 for 8 openers" math).
- Consistent with the political-intensity dial precedent (low-cardinality, set at signup, changeable, no parallel pipelines).
- Defaults CONFIRMED: weekdays only (no weekend editions), 1-5 days/wk default 5. SUMMER (June-July) auto-throttles to ONE opener/wk for everyone (cuts generation cost ~80% in those weeks since broadcast = one edition not five; keeps inbox habit; billing untouched so consistent with the no-billing-pause / summer-as-feature decision; REPLACES a pause toggle). Normal cadence resumes August (back-to-school + launch window). Deferred: summer window as operator-controlled setting not hard date; summer-school override.
- BUILT same session (Claude driving the browser into Base44): the /manage page now EXISTS IN PREVIEW (NOT published) in the production Base44 app (the one with verified caughtupai.com; "(Copy)" is a backup). Access model refined with Samuel: gate is subscriber-vs-stranger NOT paid-vs-unpaid (trial users can customize, it drives conversion); unlisted route + token in email footer (?t=manage_token); no login. Teacher entity created (full_name, email, school, delivery_days list default all 5, political_content neutral/civic, time_zone IANA, manage_token auto-unique, status trial/active/canceled) = the same record the future signup form will write. 3 test records (Sarah Chen, Marcus Webb, Priya Nair) with tokened test URLs in the Base44 chat log. ALL BEHAVIOR VERIFIED in preview: invalid/no token -> error line only; valid token -> prefilled form; save persists across reload; at-least-one-day guard; two-step in-page unsubscribe (Keep my Openers / Yes unsubscribe me); restore-subscription link works and persists. TWO BUGS found by hand-testing and fixed at source: (1) unsubscribe fired from one stray click (native confirm + buttons missing type=button submitting the form) -> replaced with in-page two-step confirm + restore link + spacing; (2) panel buttons unwired (handler after early return; state blocked behind throwing async write) -> fixed after a specific defect report. STILL OPEN before live: Samuel must Publish (his call); on the PUBLISHED app verify anonymous token-only write works AND that Teacher entity permissions are scoped (over-open perms would let anyone enumerate teacher records); wire token into signup + email footers; Resend segmentation by delivery_days + time_zone. Conversation: 20_Conversations/2026-06-10 - Delivery frequency customization.md.

**Update 2026-06-11 (PUBLISHED + live-tested; SECURITY HOLE found, hard gate):**
- Samuel published; Claude tested on the live caughtupai.com. FUNCTIONAL behavior all PASSED live (no/bad token = error only; valid token prefills; Save persists across hard reload = anonymous write works; two-step unsubscribe + restore work and persist).
- CRITICAL VULN confirmed by probe: the Teacher entity is world-readable AND world-writable with NO auth. Unauthenticated GET to app.base44.com/api/apps/6a1f286b511ece79b6ef3942/entities/Teacher returns ALL records including every full_name, email, school, status, and manage_token. Impact: PII leak of all teacher emails; total defeat of the token model (every token is handed out, so anyone can read/alter/unsubscribe any teacher); writes open on the same API as Save. Only 3 fake test records exposed so far, page unlinked, no real users -> no real breach yet, but HARD GATE before signup.
- Root cause: /manage reads+writes the Teacher table directly from the browser with public entity perms (token filtered client-side after a full fetch). FIX (architecture, not a toggle): move token lookup+update behind a Base44 backend function (browser sends only the token; server returns/updates just the one matching record), then lock the Teacher entity so the public cannot list/read/write directly; re-run the full test pass after.
- FIX BUILT IN PREVIEW (Claude drove Base44, same session): 3 backend functions (getTeacherByToken / updateTeacherByToken / setSubscriptionByToken) with service-role access, exact-token lookup, one-record ops, returning only needed fields (never id/email/manage_token); /manage rewritten to call only those functions (no direct client entity queries); Teacher entity locked rls defaultPolicy "deny". Preview prefill via getTeacherByToken works.
- NOT LIVE: lock+rewrite only apply on Publish. Re-probed PRODUCTION data API after the build: still returns all 3 records (200) -> live leak still open until Publish. Publish is gated to Samuel (auto-mode classifier blocked Claude from clicking it; live-domain deploy is the user's call). Publish deploys lock+functions+page atomically (no broken window). Only 3 fake test records exposed meanwhile; page unlinked, no users.
- NEXT (Samuel): click Publish. Then Claude re-verifies live: anonymous probe must return 0/403 AND /manage still prefills/saves/unsub+restore. Low-pri: delete the 3 fake test Teacher records.

**Update 2026-06-11 (civic pointed-pieces revisit — DISCUSSED, not confirmed):**
- After the teacher meeting, Samuel wants to reopen the earlier ban on "pointed" pieces (birthright-citizenship Open Letter that read as aimed at Trump). Belief: civic-tier teachers want pointed-but-not-aggressive pieces; AP Lang teaches persuasion and position-taking rhetoric is richest to analyze.
- Claude recommendation (Samuel had NOT explicitly accepted by session end): split pointed-at-an-ISSUE from pointed-at-a-named-PERSON. Allow issue-pointed advocacy (target = the policy) on the civic tier; keep the HARD ban on targeting named living politicians on every tier. Maps to teacher's "pointed but not aggressive" (the aggression = person-targeting). This CLARIFIES not overturns the neutrality rule (ban was always on person-targeting; issue-advocacy was over-suppressed).
- OPEN: Samuel to confirm, then update Editorial-Standards Gate 0 + feedback_opener_neutrality to explicitly permit issue-pointed advocacy on civic tier while preserving the named-living-politician ban. Also note: statusline.ps1 + settings.json statusLine wired this session (infra, see 20_Conversations/2026-06-11 - Status Line Setup.md).


---
**2026-06-11 (live security probe):** /manage read-leak FIX PARTIALLY VERIFIED on live caughtupai.com. Anonymous GET to the Teacher entity now returns `200 []` (zero records) on both app.base44.com and the caughtupai.com proxy host — the public-API data leak is CLOSED (per-op deny `{create/read/update/delete:false}` enforced). BUT the functional path FAILED ambiguously: a valid manage token shows "invalid/expired"; backend fn getTeacherByToken returns `{"found":false}`. Two unresolved causes — (1) the 3 fake test records were deleted (then leak is genuinely closed, just nothing to test) or (2) the read:false lock also blocks the service-role backend fn (over-locked = would break every real teacher link = blocker). Empty [] does NOT by itself prove a working fix. BLOCKED ON SAMUEL: did he delete the test Teacher records? Hard gate stands: nothing ships until the functional path (prefill+save+unsub/restore) is confirmed WORKING, not just until the read endpoint goes quiet. Detail: 20_Conversations/2026-06-10 - Delivery frequency customization.md.

**Update 2026-06-11 (GENERAL-ENGLISH market opening — teacher feedback; DECIDED direction, NOT built):**
- Teacher's third meeting point: the system would work for regular (non-AP) English teachers too; he says nothing needs to change except the AP-Lang-specific LABELS on the Openers, the rest is great English practice regardless of AP.
- Claude's read (mostly agrees): "just labels" is ~80% right. Student-facing Opener content transfers as-is (the daily nonfiction passage + close reading + MCQs + SOAPSTone headnote are general ELA practice; neutrality + plain-knowledge gates already target "a high schooler" not AP-elite). TWO things that are MORE than labels: (1) the teacher-copy SKILL TAXONOMY (it tags "AP reading skills" + an "AP alignment" section; for general classes these map to plain reading/analysis skills or Common Core ELA, a real swap); (2) READING LEVEL/RIGOR — the prose is deliberately AP-caliber in sentence craft, fine for 11-12 but a possible stretch for 9th/lower-track. The ONE thing to verify with a general-English teacher before scaling (the content is accessible; the sentences are sophisticated by design).
- Strategic significance: TAM is ~an order of magnitude bigger (AP Lang is a narrow wedge; general secondary English is the whole population).
- DECISION (Samuel, via forced choice): KEEP AP LANG AS THE LAUNCH WEDGE (sharp, ownable, word-of-mouth inside a defined community; do not dilute the launch message). Build the general-English support as a FAST-FOLLOW after AP Lang has traction.
- BUILD MODEL (planned, not built): "audience: AP Lang / General English" becomes one more CUSTOMIZATION DIMENSION, exactly like the political-intensity dial and delivery frequency. Student Opener stays IDENTICAL; the toggle swaps only (a) teacher-copy skill tags + the "AP alignment" section -> general reading/analysis or Common Core framing, and (b) the marketing/signup framing. NO second content pipeline, NO rebuild. Implementation surface when built: teacher-copy render (skill tags + AP-alignment section), MCQ-Construction-Spec (AP reading skills -> general skills), marketing/signup copy.
- OPEN action (Samuel): verify reading-level fit with a general-English teacher before scaling the expansion. Not building the toggle now (post-launch fast-follow).


---
**2026-06-11 (security fix RESOLVED):** /manage read-leak fix VERIFIED DONE on live caughtupai.com. Confirmed all 3 Teacher records still exist in the Base44 data grid (earlier found:false was over-lock, not deletion). Working architecture: entity locked admin-only `{create/read/update/delete:false}` + backend functions (getTeacherByToken / updateTeacherByToken / setSubscriptionByToken) access data via `base44.asServiceRole.entities.Teacher`. Live re-probe: anonymous `entities/Teacher` = `200 []` (leak closed, stays closed after writes); getTeacherByToken returns Marcus (found:true); /manage prefills correctly; Save round-trip persists across hard reload (toggled Wed on/off, reverted clean). Read+write both work through the lock. NOTE: the Base44 builder false-alarms that the leak persists — it tests with admin credentials so it always sees all rows; ignore its "platform bug / contact support" advice. Cleanup (low pri): delete the 3 fake test records eventually. HARD GATE CLEARED — /manage safe to wire into the real signup/email flow. Detail: 20_Conversations/2026-06-10 - Delivery frequency customization.md.

**2026-06-11 (GENERAL-ENGLISH EXPANSION BUILT, brought forward from fast-follow):** Samuel asked to build the general-English expansion (teacher-feedback item #3) and add it to the Base44 /manage page now. DONE in two halves. (1) CONTENT: new spec General-English-Mode-Spec.md (D11) defines the `audience` dimension (ap_lang default | general_english) + the AP-reading-skill -> general/Common Core map; render_opener_v2.py parameterized with an `audience` field that swaps ONLY the masthead tagline, MCQ header, writing-type label (Q1/Q2/Q3 -> plain names), and the alignment section (AP-exam framing -> Reading-and-analysis framing from a new `gen_alignment` field); dual-write (`gen_alignment`/`gen_type`) wired into caughtupai-sampleprompt-V1 + MCQ-Construction-Spec. Verified by rendering the same piece both ways: labels swap, all student pedagogical content byte-identical. Design note: the masthead tagline + MCQ header + writing-type label DO swap on the student copy too (so a general handout never says "AP-style"/"Q3"); "student identical" means identical LEARNING content. (2) PAGE (Base44, PREVIEW only, not published): drove the in-app builder to add an `audience` field to the Teacher entity (lock CONFIRMED intact `{create/read/update/delete:false}` in the Permissions panel), thread it through getTeacherByToken (returns it, default ap_lang) + updateTeacherByToken (validates ap_lang|general_english, persists via asServiceRole), and add a prefilled "Course" radio group to /manage (AP English Language / General English (non-AP)) in the Save payload with helper text. All four pieces code-verified in the editor. OPEN: Samuel clicks Publish (his gated call), then live re-verify (anonymous leak probe still `[]`, /manage prefills+saves audience). Still to do before MARKETING the expansion: confirm reading-level fit with a general-English teacher; AP Lang stays the launch wedge; signup/marketing audience copy not built yet. Detail: 20_Conversations/2026-06-11 - General English expansion build.md + General-English-Mode-Spec.md.

---
**2026-06-11 (GENERATION PIPELINE BUILT as the beta engine):** Samuel reframed the on-demand Opener prompt as the real product beta and asked to build the production generation pipeline now. BUILT: an enforced, model-routed workflow at `.claude/workflows/caughtup-opener.mjs` (3 phases: Verify on Haiku = pick a real story from last 5 days + web-confirm every fact + sourced fact list; Compose on FABLE 5 = read all binding spec files, headnote-first, draft, self-run the 4 gates with the friction/anti-symmetry layer, build devices + 2 MCQs + apparatus, emit a schema-valid piece dict; Render on Haiku = write the dict to JSON + a driver that imports build()/randomize_answers() from render_opener_v2.py UNMODIFIED, produce teacher + student PDFs, append to a new editions-log.tsv). Model routing is ENFORCED per phase (opts.model), not advisory; this is what Samuel meant by building it for real. Data contract locked by a JSON schema mirroring render_opener_v2's piece dict (the [[n]] device marks, devices/mcq/writing shapes) so Compose can't drift from what the renderer consumes. Recency is now persistent via editions-log.tsv (Compose reads it to avoid repeating the last register/template/opener/close). Scope decided (AskUserQuestion): generation pipeline ONLY this pass; no mid-run human approval; email-PDF delivery + scheduling deferred. VERIFIED END TO END: first run produced both PDFs (~850KB each) in ~8.5 min / ~193k tokens, fully unattended; story = 2026 US measles totals (The Ledger); facts INDEPENDENTLY re-confirmed by Claude against CDC/CIDRAP/CNN (2,030 cases as of June 4; Spartanburg 997, over Apr 26; 945/990 unvaccinated) = the Haiku verify pass was accurate. Trigger by asking "build today's Opener" (optional topic/register args). Beta caveat: still eyeball facts before any real teacher until it holds across a batch (the human-panel discipline). Detail: 10_Projects/Caught Up AI/Generation-Pipeline.md + 20_Conversations/2026-06-11 - Opener Generation Pipeline.md.
